content.problemStatement:
There are NNN magnets on a tree of NNN nodes numbered 111 to NNN, one in each node.

Upon introducing an external magnet at some node of the tree, all the magnets come closer by 111 step, while the magnets at that node remains stationary. More formally, suppose we introduced an external magnet at node xxx, then :


Magnets placed at node xxx will not move.
Magnets placed at any other node u(‚âÝx)u (\ne x)u(ÓÄÝ=x) will move to the unique node vvv such that

the path from uuu to xxx passes through vvv
vvv is adjacent to uuu.


Note that after all the NNN magnets have moved, we remove the external magnet we had introduced.

You want to introduce and remove NNN total external magnets, at each of the vertices 111 to NNN. But, you can choose the order in which you will introduce the magnet at each node.

Is it possible to choose a valid order such that all magnets do **not** end up in the same vertex at the end? Construct any valid order if possible.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the number of nodes in the tree
The next N‚àí1N - 1N‚àí1 lines contains uuu and vvv representing an edge of the tree.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line :



‚àí1-1‚àí1 if no valid order is possible.
P1,P2,....,PNP_1, P_2, ...., P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,....,PN‚Äã representing a valid order, which must form a permutation of the integers 111 to NNN.
constraints: The given set of edges describes a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 : It can be proven that it is impossible.
Test case 2 : Let's call the magnet initially at node XXX as MXM_XMX‚Äã. We will track where the magnets go after each external magnet is introduced and removed

External magnet at 333 : M1:2M_1 : 2M1‚Äã:2, M2:3M_2 : 3M2‚Äã:3, M3:3M_3 : 3M3‚Äã:3, M4:3M_4 : 3M4‚Äã:3
External magnet at 444 : M1:3M_1 : 3M1‚Äã:3, M2:4M_2 : 4M2‚Äã:4, M3:4M_3 : 4M3‚Äã:4, M4:4M_4 : 4M4‚Äã:4
External magnet at 222 : M1:2M_1 : 2M1‚Äã:2, M2:3M_2 : 3M2‚Äã:3, M3:3M_3 : 3M3‚Äã:3, M4:3M_4 : 3M4‚Äã:3
External magnet at 111 : M1:1M_1 : 1M1‚Äã:1, M2:2M_2 : 2M2‚Äã:2, M3:2M_3 : 2M3‚Äã:2, M4:2M_4 : 2M4‚Äã:2

Thus, we can see that all the magnets did not end up in the same spot. 333 of them ended up at 222 and 111 of them at node 111.
